programs.title: History's Greatest Warriors
programs.desc: The Mongol's unmatched skill in horseback archery and lightning-fast mobility across vast terrains build him into the most elite fighter of his time.

programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.desc: Known for their seafaring skills and feared for their ferocity, the Vikings amassed gold, silver and other riches while trading and raiding across Europe; catches of this treasure have been found in Europe and North America, but this may be just the tip of the iceberg; are there any rich Viking hoards still waiting to be found? And if so, where are they?

programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.desc: On December 11, 1978, a gang of thieves lifted around $5 million in cash and $800,000 in jewelry from a luxury airline hangar at New York's JFK Airport, marking the largest heist in U.S. history at that time. The authorities interview employees and find a connection to local mafia bosses... but every time they close in on a potential suspect, that suspect ends up dead. Almost 50 years later, they still haven't found all the culprits-or the goods. So, who orchestrated the Lufthansa heist?

programs.title: History's Greatest Warriors
programs.desc: The Mongol's unmatched skill in horseback archery and lightning-fast mobility across vast terrains build him into the most elite fighter of his time.

programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.desc: In a perfectly planned robbery, thieves steal half a billion dollars in masterpieces from the Gardner Museum in Boston; it's been three decades since the brazen heist, and an exhaustive search has failed to recover the stolen goods.

programs.title: History's Greatest Mysteries
programs.desc: Known for their seafaring skills and feared for their ferocity, the Vikings amassed gold, silver and other riches while trading and raiding across Europe; catches of this treasure have been found in Europe and North America, but this may be just the tip of the iceberg; are there any rich Viking hoards still waiting to be found? And if so, where are they?
